words.sentence: My head started to ache after I read for too long.
The lion got ready to attack its prey.
We found old toys in the dusty attic.
Cut the pizza into equal slices for everyone.
The earthquake made the dishes rattle on the shelf.
I put the coin into my jacket pocket.
We met at the public library after school.
Raise your hand if you have a question.
Climbing without a rope is too risky.
My stomach growled because I was hungry.
Mom put a bandage on my scraped knee.
I took a quick glance at the clock.
Reading books gives you a lot of knowledge.
We danced at my aunt's marriage celebration.
I left a message on Grandma's phone.
Don't misjudge a book by its cover.
Did you notice the new sign by the door?
A big package arrived for me today.
I have soccer practice after school.
Science class is my favorite subject.

lists.title: Module 6 Week 3 — Spelling (prefixes re-, un-, dis-)

words.sentence: My friend and I sometimes disagree about games.
It is not safe to disobey the crossing guard.
The coach looked displeased when we lost the game.
After the storm, we had to rebuild the treehouse.
I had to redo my homework because I lost it.
A cool drink will refresh you on a hot day.
She was undaunted by the long climb up the hill.
The library bill is still unpaid.
Our trip to the park was unplanned but fun.
